contractTitle: Proof of Concept / Pilot Engagement Agreement between Coastal Elements AI and S.L. Nusbaum

provider.name: Coastal Elements AI (with poly186.io as technical/delivery partner)

contacts.name: Dylan Ryan
contacts.notes: Primary business and account lead; based in Virginia Beach / Hampton Roads. Will schedule follow‑ups and send workshop materials.

contacts.name: Princeps Polycap
contacts.notes: Leads AI/LLM architecture, infrastructure, model selection, and implementation; focuses on Azure/OpenAI/Claude, process automation, and fine‑tuning.

contacts.name: Drew
contacts.notes: Coordinates teams, timelines, resources, and client communications for implementations.

contactsDisplay: Dylan Ryan — Founder / Principal; primary account lead
Princeps Polycap — Technical Lead (poly186.io); AI/LLM architecture

contacts.name: Richard
contacts.notes: Existing relationship with Dylan and Mark; made the introduction; likely a stakeholder among brokers in future workshops.

client.name: S.L. Nusbaum

contacts.name: Mark Ashley
contacts.notes: Primary client sponsor and decision maker for AI/IT initiatives; manages small IT team supporting ~200 corporate and ~1,000 multifamily employees; wants governance, Azure‑native solutions, and marketable AI offerings for clients.

contactsDisplay: Mark Ashley — IT Director; primary sponsor and decision maker

deliverables: Initial AI readiness and process/data audit focusing on key departments (HR, Finance/Accounting, Multifamily Operations, Brokerage).
Workshop framework document outlining how to run AI discovery sessions with department heads (including agendas and facilitation approach).
Department‑specific discovery question lists to uncover AI/automation use cases and pain points.
ROI‑oriented analysis template for AI use cases (time spent, manual effort, process fragmentation, expected savings/value).
Recommendation summary of potential AI use cases, prioritized by ROI and strategic value (especially client‑facing, marketable offerings).
Design and potential build of AI solutions in S.L. Nusbaum’s Azure environment (e.g., contract review assistant, process automation, fine‑tuned models) after January follow‑up and scoping.
Optional access/integration to Coastal Elements’ existing real‑estate AI SaaS solutions where appropriate.

phases.name: Phase 1 – Discovery & AI Readiness
phases.description: Understand S.L. Nusbaum’s current AI usage, infrastructure, departmental needs, and executive priorities; identify quick wins and strategically valuable client‑facing opportunities.
activities: Conduct high‑level AI/process/data audit (Azure, M365/Copilot, existing bots).
Hold one‑on‑one discussions with executives and department heads using workshop framework and questions.
Map KPIs/OKRs to potential AI/automation improvements.
Identify manual/administrative tasks suitable for automation (contract review, data aggregation, reporting).
dependencies: Mark completes internal conversations with executives, department heads, and brokers (Nov–Jan).
Workshop question framework and guidance from Coastal Elements AI provided to Mark.
outputs: List of departmental pain points and AI/automation opportunities.
Preliminary inventory of candidate use cases.

phases.name: Phase 2 – Use Case Prioritization & POC Design
phases.description: Translate discovery findings into prioritized AI initiatives and define one or more POCs, with clear ROI and governance.
activities: Evaluate use cases against KPIs/OKRs and ROI potential.
Decide whether to leverage vendor features, Coastal Elements SaaS, or custom Azure builds.
Design POC(s) including data sources, models, and security constraints.
Estimate costs, resources, timelines; define success metrics and governance.
dependencies: Executive clarity on client‑facing vs internal solutions.
outputs: POC design documents (architecture, data flows, model choices, security/governance).
Initial budgetary estimates and ROI justification narrative.

phases.name: Phase 3 – POC Build & Deployment (Client Environment)
phases.description: Implement selected AI POC(s) in the Client’s Azure tenant, integrating with existing systems.
activities: Stand up required Azure services and AI infrastructure in Nusbaum’s environment.
Ingest and preprocess internal documents for LLM use with proper access controls.
Build automations and agents (contract assistant, broker briefing generator, process bots).
Fine‑tune or configure models based on Nusbaum’s documents where beneficial.
User acceptance testing and iterative prompt/system instruction tuning.
Handover documentation and light training for IT and end users.
dependencies: Green‑light and budget approval from executive committee based on Phase 2 outputs.
Azure access and security/governance approvals from Mark and IT team.
outputs: Working POC(s) deployed in Nusbaum’s Azure environment (or hosted as agreed).
Usage and performance metrics against defined KPIs/OKRs.

phases.name: Phase 4 – Scale‑up & Marketable Offering
phases.description: Scale successful POCs into production and position them as part of Nusbaum’s client‑facing offerings.
activities: Harden POC solutions for production (monitoring, logging, support).
Expand rollout across departments/properties where applicable.
Assist in articulating AI offerings for marketing and sales.
Provide ongoing advisory to avoid snowflake technical debt.
dependencies: Successful POC(s) with demonstrated ROI and executive sponsorship.
Defined go‑to‑market message for AI‑enabled services.
outputs: Production AI solutions embedded in operations and client offerings.
Executive‑level AI value story and collateral.

phaseSummaries: Phase 1 – Discovery & AI Readiness: audit, interviews, KPI mapping, quick wins.
Phase 2 – Use Case Prioritization & POC Design: prioritize, design POC(s), estimate costs.
Phase 3 – POC Build & Deployment: implement POC(s) in Azure, test, handover.
Phase 4 – Scale‑up & Marketable Offering: production hardening, rollout, go‑to‑market support.

timeline.duration: Exploratory phase through mid‑January 2026, with POC and implementation timelines to be determined after discovery.

milestones.name: Internal Discovery by Mark with Execs & Departments
milestones.details: Mark to meet with executives, department heads, and brokers to gather AI needs, pain points, and opportunities.

milestones.name: Mid‑January Follow‑Up Meeting
milestones.details: Regroup between Coastal Elements AI and S.L. Nusbaum to review discovery insights and identify candidate POCs.

milestones.name: Workshop Framework & Questions Delivery
milestones.targetDate: Within ~1 week after the November 5 meeting
milestones.details: Dylan to email Mark example workshop framework and department‑specific discovery questions.

milestones.name: Potential POC Kickoff
milestones.details: To be scheduled post mid‑January meeting, once prioritized use cases and budget are defined.

milestonesSummaries: Internal discovery by Mark with execs & departments — by mid‑January.
Mid‑January follow‑up meeting to review discovery insights.
Delivery of workshop framework & questions within ~1 week after Nov 5 meeting.
Potential POC kickoff to be scheduled after mid‑January.

commercialTerms.paymentTerms: Phase 1: $20,000 (50% due on signing, 50% due on delivery of Phase 1 report). Phase 2: $12,000 (50% on Phase 2 start, 50% on delivery of POC design). Phase 3: POC build to be estimated and approved; billed T&M or fixed fee as agreed. Invoices net 30. Travel/expenses reimbursed at cost with prior approval.
commercialTerms.estimatedValue: Initial discovery & design fixed fees approx. $32,000; POC build estimates TBD after Phase 2.
commercialTerms.roiExpectation: Engagements must show clear ROI; Coastal will quantify ROI by comparing current manual time vs. post‑automation time and tying improvements to existing KPIs/OKRs.

nextSteps.action: Schedule a mid‑January follow‑up meeting with Mark to review departmental conversations and AI opportunities.
Email Mark a workshop framework plus discovery questions for key departments (HR, Finance/Accounting, Multifamily, Brokerage).
nextSteps.source: I'm going to... put together a couple things just to send over...

nextSteps.action: Conduct one‑on‑one discussions with executives and department heads to understand their AI needs and ideas.
nextSteps.source: I'm starting to have more conversations with some of the department heads...

nextSteps.action: Clarify executive committee expectations for AI and identify potential high‑value use cases.
nextSteps.source: it needs to be something that they can go out there in the field...

nextSteps.action: Schedule an informal lunch before January follow‑up to strengthen relationship and discuss AI ideas.

keyQuotes: My focus has been... we have Copilot, we've released it to everyone...
To me, it is... I tend to look at what time is being spent... shows an ROI there immediately.
I really think that a partnership between our groups... focusing on developing a solution... market what AI solutions we have.
nextStepsSummaries: Dylan to schedule mid‑January follow‑up meeting with Mark.
Dylan to email workshop framework and discovery questions to Mark.
Mark to conduct one‑on‑one discussions with execs and department heads.
Mark to clarify executive expectations for client‑facing AI offerings.
Informal lunch meeting to strengthen relationships and discuss ideas.
